name: Infinite Financial Firm
description_short: Infinite Financial Firm is a Los Angeles-based credit repair document preparation service that helps clients dispute inaccurate items on credit reports through a 3-step process.
description_long:
Infinite Financial Firm is a privately owned document preparation and tracking service based in Los Angeles, California, with 10 years of claimed industry experience. The company specializes in credit repair document preparation, positioning itself as a service that helps consumers challenge inaccurate or unverifiable information on their credit reports rather than providing direct credit counseling or legal services.

The company offers a structured 3-step process: (1) Review of the client's credit report, (2) Preparation and submission of dispute documents to credit bureaus and creditors, and (3) Ongoing monitoring through a client portal. They claim that on average, clients see 7% of negative items removed monthly, with most clients experiencing results within 30-45 days. Services include dispute document preparation for charge-offs, bankruptcies, loan denials, and other negative credit items. They also mention a client portal for tracking progress and access to additional resources for paying members.

What distinguishes Infinite Financial Firm is their emphasis on personalized, 1-on-1 service tailored to individual credit situations. They highlight a willingness to resubmit documents if initial disputes don't result in changes, and they provide a client portal for self-monitoring. The company explicitly states it is bonded and positions itself as understanding the specific documentation and rules required by credit bureaus.

However, potential clients should note several limitations. The company makes specific performance claims (7% monthly removal rate, 30-45 day results) that likely vary significantly by individual circumstance. As stated in their disclaimer, they do not guarantee specific outcomes, and results may vary. Consumers can dispute credit report errors independently without paying for document preparation services. The website lacks transparent pricing information, and the service is positioned as document preparation assistance rather than actual dispute filing on the consumer's behalf.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Bonded credit repair document preparation company with 10 years claimed industry experience
Claims average 7% of negative items removed per month with most results in 30-45 days
Offers personalized 1-on-1 service tailored to individual credit situations
Provides secure client portal for ongoing credit monitoring and tracking
Will resubmit dispute documents if initial challenges don't result in changes
Handles diverse credit issues including charge-offs, bankruptcies, and loan denials
Located in Los Angeles with multiple customer testimonials showing significant score increases (103-122 points)
cons: Makes specific performance claims (7% monthly removal, 30-45 day results) that likely vary significantly and are not guaranteed
No transparent pricing information available on website; costs unclear upfront
Consumers can dispute credit errors independently at no cost—paying for document preparation may be unnecessary
Disclaimer clearly states company does not guarantee specific financial outcomes and results may vary
Positioned as document preparation assistance rather than actually filing disputes on consumer's behalf, limiting service scope
best_for: Consumers with multiple negative items (charge-offs, collections, bankruptcies) seeking structured guidance on dispute process
Los Angeles-area residents who prefer local, personalized service with 1-on-1 support
People who want ongoing monitoring and portal access to track credit repair progress
Clients willing to pay for professional document preparation rather than self-filing disputes
diagnosis: Infinite Financial Firm is best suited for Los Angeles consumers with multiple negative credit items who want professional assistance preparing dispute documents and prefer guided, personalized service with ongoing monitoring. The critical caveat is that the company does not actually file disputes—it prepares documents for consumers to submit themselves—and consumers can dispute inaccurate items independently at no cost; paying for document preparation is optional and results are not guaranteed.
services: Dispute document preparation for inaccurate credit report items
Resubmission of dispute letters if initial challenges unsuccessful
Secure client portal for credit monitoring
Access to additional credit resources for paying members
Credit score tracking and progress monitoring
Dispute letter creation for credit bureaus and creditors
Third-party credit report pulling through affiliates
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 1722 Westwood Blvd Suite 200, Los Angeles, CA 90024
